payload.text: 按照这个地形参考图，用banana帮我生成一张游戏地图，借鉴全面战争：三国和战锤的地图设计，将建筑物、资源点和地图背景分开

**Designing Game World Details**

I'm currently fleshing out the game's world, drawing inspiration from "Xuan Jian Xian Zu" and "Gui Gu Ba Huang." I've visualized a regional map of "Wangyue Lake," marking clan territories like Li, Shao, Wei, and Beihan Sect. My next step involves leveraging the Gemini API to dynamically update and refine this map.


**Refining Visual Style**

I'm now focused on the map's visual style, aiming for a detailed, stylized look reminiscent of "Total War" and "Warhammer" games. My goal is to use `gemini-2.5-flash-image` by default, upgrading to `gemini-3-pro-image-preview` for higher quality. I need to design prompts that help the model generate a version of the provided map that resembles a high-end strategy game map. My code is partially laid out, including `index.html` with Tailwind, fonts, and basic CSS; `index.tsx` as an entry point; `App.tsx` for state; and `components/WorldMap.tsx` for rendering, currently with a CSS-based parchment background.


**Prioritizing Image Generation**

I've realized that the user's request is best served by using image generation. My priority is shifting towards implementing a "Map Generation Service." This service will take a base64 encoded reference image (derived from the user's prompt) and pass it to Gemini to generate the map. I'll add a "Refresh Map" feature in the world map component to trigger this process. The prompt for the image model will start with "A highly detailed fantasy strategy game map of".


**Visualizing Map Updates**

I'm now fully immersed in the "Total War" and "Warhammer" aesthetic for Wangyue Lake. I'm focusing on incorporating a large central blue lake surrounded by visually distinct territories, such as the Li Clan, Shao Clan, Wei Clan, and Beihan Sect. I'm aiming for a hand-drawn artistic style with mist, mountains, and forests in 4K resolution from an aerial perspective. My immediate code focus involves updating `services/geminiService.ts` to include `generateGameMap` and updating `components/WorldMap.tsx` to handle generation triggering and display. I'm also planning to add a loading state. My previous self-correction clarified the "banana" models.


**Developing Render Logic**

I'm now focusing on integrating the image generation into the map display. I'll update `components/WorldMap.tsx` to handle the generated background image. Also, I'm integrating a "Re-generate Map" button to `App.tsx` for easy map refreshing. I'll refine the visual representation with a detailed prompt, now aiming for a 'Total War' aesthetic with ink-wash details and stylized icons. I plan to use `gemini-2.5-flash-image` by default. The component's architecture will keep the map in the background and UI buttons layered on top, consistent with my existing design.


**Generating Layered Map Elements**

I've realized the user's focus is on layered elements, not just a static image. I'll focus the image generation on the terrain and regional elements as a background. I'm now modifying `services/geminiService.ts` to accommodate the `backgroundImage` state. My new goal is to allow the user to illuminate the map using the Gemini API. This means my code will use `gemini-2.5-flash-image` and call `ai.models.generateContent` with the prompt. The resultant images will contain `inlineData`. I'm starting work on these updates now.

entries.description: 添加基于参考图生成游戏地图的功能，使用 gemini-2.5-flash-image 模型。
更新 WorldMap 组件，支持动态生成的地图背景图，并优化 UI 层次结构。
